title: Security Agent Output Schema
description: Structured output schema for the Security review agent. This schema defines the expected format for security review results, including findings, recommendations, and overall security score.

findings.description: List of security findings identified in the architecture

title.description: Clear, concise title of the security finding
title.example: Missing encryption in transit for API calls

description.description: Detailed description of the security issue, why it matters, and its implications
description.example: API communication between microservices uses HTTP without TLS encryption. This exposes authentication tokens and sensitive data to man-in-the-middle attacks.

severity.description: Severity level of this finding based on exploitability and impact

affected_components.description: List of architecture components affected by this finding

recommendations.description: List of actionable security recommendations to improve the architecture

title.example: Implement TLS 1.3 encryption for all service-to-service communication

description.description: Detailed explanation of the recommendation, why it matters, and expected benefits
description.example: Enable mutual TLS (mTLS) with automatic certificate rotation between all microservices to encrypt traffic and authenticate service identity.

severity.description: Priority level of implementing this recommendation

affected_components.description: Components that need to implement this recommendation

security_score.description: Overall security score for the architecture on a scale from 0.0 (critically insecure) to 1.0 (exemplary security). Use 2 decimal places (e.g., 0.75, 0.82).

confidence.description: Your confidence level in this assessment (0.0-1.0). Lower confidence may indicate incomplete information or architectural ambiguity.

summary.description: Brief summary of the security posture and key concerns
summary.example: Strong security foundation with encryption gaps in service-to-service communication and missing rate limiting on APIs.
